title: pilosella floribunda subsp. regiomontana
intro: Pilosella floribunda subsp. regiomontana, also known as the mountain mouse-ear, is a flowering plant species in the dandelion family (Asteraceae). This plant is known for its potential medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral effects, making it a valuable herb in traditional medicine. The health benefits of Pilosella floribunda subsp. regiomontana include its ability to treat wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, allowing for a range of applications in natural remedies and homeopathic treatments.
